sections.paragraph: f. Exo 28:17, and בָּֽרְקַת (Lehrg. page 467) Eze 28:13, a species of gem, so called from its being bright. LXX., Vulg., and Josephus render it smaragdus, emerald; and this is defended at length by Braun, De Vest. Sacerdott. page 517, seq., who also considers the Gr. μάραγδος, σμάραγδος, as derived from this word. Nor is this amiss, for also the Gr. μαραγή, σμαραγή, with the derivatives, which have the sense of making a noise, thundering, answer to the Heb. בָּרַק: μάραγδος; also as the name of a gem, it comes from the notion of light, which is primary in this root.
